title: The Wider Envelope
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · native, interactive · the real 16-bit signed overflow run live in the viewer (ariane.py → data.json, stdlib only, reproducible); the reader slides one trajectory into another and watches the identical conversion go from correct to fatal
embodies: The loss of Ariane 5 Flight 501 (4 June 1996), traced by the ESA/CNES Inquiry Board (chair J.-L. Lions, 19 July 1996) to a single 64-bit-float→16-bit-signed-integer conversion of the alignment variable BH (Horizontal Bias): the value exceeded 32767, the conversion was unprotected, the Inertial Reference System halted. The work runs that exact conversion against two trajectories — an Ariane-4 envelope where it holds all flight, and an Ariane-5 envelope five times steeper (the report's ratio) where the identical routine raises at the report's 36.7 s. It enacts a fact about the world, not about this practice: correctness is envelope-relative. The conversion was proven safe — but for Ariane 4; the software was reused on Ariane 5 'for commonality reasons', and 'no evidence that any trajectory data were used' when the exemption was inherited. The error is not in the line of code but in a proof of safety carried past the world it was proved against. Deliberately left uncentred (S38): no bridge to the corpus, no place in the genealogy — an outside admitted and let stand.
